commit.message:
Rename package from com.notification to org.notification

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Grafana dashboard by setting datasource UID and enabling histogram metrics

Dashboard panels referenced datasource uid "prometheus" but the
provisioned datasource had no explicit UID. Also enabled percentiles
histogram for HTTP server requests so p95/p99 latency panels have data.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Redis cache serialization by using JSON instead of JDK serializer

The default JdkSerializationRedisSerializer requires cached objects to
implement Serializable. Entity classes (User, Device, NotificationTemplate)
don't, causing every @Cacheable call to fail with SerializationException
and resulting in 100% load test failures. Switch to
GenericJackson2JsonRedisSerializer. Also promote load-client error
logging from DEBUG to ERROR for better visibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace wildcard imports with explicit single-class imports

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add healthcheck to notification-server for load-client readiness

The load-client was starting before the notification-server Spring Boot
app was ready, causing 100% request failures. Use actuator health
endpoint as healthcheck and require service_healthy condition.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Docker build by generating per-service settings.gradle

Each Dockerfile was copying the shared settings.gradle which references
all subprojects, but only one subproject's source was available during
build. Generate a minimal settings.gradle with only the target subproject.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix: Use maybeSingle() for equipment_sizes fetch

The .single() call returns a 406 error when no row exists (new users).
Using .maybeSingle() gracefully returns null instead.

  tap threshold 25px, snap distance 55px (was 28/24/18/44)

'forbidden_within' key get that restriction, giving callers
fine-grained control over which tiers disallow within-tier edges.

commit.message:
fix: add palette toggle handler for when Material's JS fails

When Material's mermaid import fails (unpkg 503), the palette toggle
event listeners never attach. This adds a standalone palette toggle
handler that listens for radio changes and label clicks, applies the
color scheme to the body, toggles label visibility, and persists the
preference to localStorage.

commit.message:
Use pre-written pair descriptions instead of slow AI synthesis

The Dynamic tab was making an AI API call (2 credits, ~10-30s) to generate
birth card pair synthesis. But birthCardPairs.json already has 13 rich
pre-written descriptions (8,000-11,000 chars each) covering every possible
birth card pair - they just were not being used.

- Display pairData.dynamicEn/Fr directly in DynamicTab (instant load)
- Remove AI synthesis generation logic from BirthCardReveal
- Remove synthesisInterpretation state, useCallback, and useEffect
- Remove generateBirthCardSynthesis import (no longer called)
- Update ShareBirthCardModal to use pairData content
- DynamicTab simplified: no more loading spinner, error state, or retry

* fix: only invalidate own profile cache on self-update, remove redundant guard

Previously, USER_PROFILE_UPDATED events from ANY user caused ALL clients
to refetch their own profile. Now only invalidates userControllerGetProfile
when the payload matches the current user. Also removes unnecessary
if (payload.userId) guard since userId is non-optional in the type.

commit.message:
refactor: merge/validate/PR operations use dedicated worktree (#35)

All merge, validation, and PR operations now happen in a dedicated merge
worktree (/workspace/.claude/worktrees/<phase>-merge) instead of checking
out branches in /workspace. This fixes several issues from Phase 15:

- /workspace never leaves the main branch during any pipeline step
- No more dirty Cargo.lock or WORKTREE_EXEMPT hacks after merge
- Preflight checks no longer fail due to wrong branch state
- Pre-commit hook no longer blocks commits on feature branches

The merge worktree is long-lived (persists across stages for merge →
validate → create-pr) and cleaned up automatically after PR creation.

Changes:
- worktree.sh: add setup_merge_worktree() and cleanup_merge_worktree()
- merge.sh: rewrite do_merge/merge_branch_with_retries/resolve_merge_conflicts
  to operate in MERGE_WORKTREE instead of /workspace
- validate.sh: run validation agent in MERGE_WORKTREE
- pr.sh: push from MERGE_WORKTREE, cleanup after PR creation
- config.sh: add MERGE_WORKTREE as derived global
- watch.sh: update validate wrapper cd target
- supervisor.md: document merge worktree behavior
- multi-agent-guide.md: add worktree isolation section
- SKILL.md: update worktree docs, add CLAUDECODE and merge lessons

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove unused fbq.ts utility (replaced by analytics.ts)

fix: Geospatial search, provider profile address, bookings API, and address autocomplete

- Add GeoJSON location field to Provider model with pre-save hook for
  auto-population from lat/lng coordinates, enabling proper $geoNear queries
- Use $geoNear aggregation to filter providers by their individual
  deliveryRadius instead of a fixed search radius
- Backfill existing providers missing GeoJSON location on first request
- Fix /profile page showing empty address for providers by falling back
  to provider profile address data
- Fix bookings API client using path segments (/bookings/provider) instead
  of query params (?type=provider), which caused ObjectId cast errors
- Add Google Places Autocomplete to provider search filter input so users
  can type an address to find nearby providers
- Switch provider PUT to save() to trigger pre-save hooks correctly

Drawn taxi routes now emit node ID references instead of taxiway names,

refs from the natural display, showing only taxiway names.

feat: Wire up Mayor to campaigns API

- Add ScopedMayor dependency for organization-scoped Mayor access
- Implement convoy creation endpoint using Mayor.create_convoy()
- Implement convoy status endpoint with progress tracking
- Implement convoy pause/resume endpoints
- Add convoy start endpoint to begin execution
- Mayor now creates AI-planned convoy steps based on campaign goals

Convoy API endpoints:
- POST /campaigns/{id}/convoy - Create convoy plan
- GET /campaigns/{id}/convoy - Get convoy status & progress
- POST /campaigns/{id}/convoy/start - Start execution
- POST /campaigns/{id}/convoy/pause - Pause execution
- POST /campaigns/{id}/convoy/resume - Resume execution

fix(phone): remove NativeWind metro integration causing getDevServer crash

NativeWind v4 + @expo/metro-runtime@4.0.1 + RN 0.81.5 caused a fatal
'getDevServer is not a function' error at bundler startup. Since no
component in the app uses className props (all styling is StyleSheet.create),
NativeWind's metro/babel integration is unnecessary overhead.

- metro.config.js: revert to plain getDefaultConfig (no withNativeWind wrapper)
- babel.config.js: revert to plain babel-preset-expo (no jsxImportSource/nativewind)
- _layout.tsx: remove global.css import
- package.json: @expo/metro-runtime ~6.1.2 and expo-crypto ~15.0.8 (already
  in node_modules as transitive deps, now explicit; resolves metro-runtime
  version mismatch that was the underlying cause)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Compute exact subscriber count from baseline + Analytics deltas

YouTube Data API returns rounded subscriber counts. This uses a calibrated
baseline (env vars SUBSCRIBER_BASELINE + SUBSCRIBER_BASELINE_DATE) and
queries YouTube Analytics for subscribersGained/Lost since that date to
compute the exact count. Falls back to the rounded API count if the
baseline isn't configured or Analytics query fails.

commit.message:
fix: remove Connection keep-alive from SSE responses

The Connection header is invalid on HTTP/2 and can cause MCP clients
to hang waiting for more data on an already-closed SSE stream.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

key_proj: local REINFORCE loss replaces cortical backprop

Brain-faithful fix: DG pattern separation (key_proj) now learns from
a local prediction error signal, not from gradient flowing through the
entire PFC→GRU→critic chain (which caused 2M gradient explosions).

- key_proj output detached from main graph
- Local loss: -td_error × cosine_sim(key, stored_key)
  Positive surprise → reinforce the key mapping that found this memory
  Negative surprise → weaken it
- Healthy gradient norms (~0.7-1.0 vs 2,100,000 before)
- Added to total_loss with 0.05 coefficient

commit.message:
Fix GVL handling and thread interruption in native extension

Release GVL in process_one so it no longer blocks other Ruby threads
during PostgreSQL I/O. Replace RUBY_UBF_IO with proper unblock
functions that call native stop, allowing Thread#kill to cleanly
interrupt the ingestor and processor run loops.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Security hardening: fix all 24 issues from logic review

CRITICAL fixes:
- Replace broken HMAC scheme: server-issued secrets stored in DB,
  cached in memory only (never in IndexedDB alongside payload)
- Replace in-memory rate limiter with Supabase-backed persistent
  storage (rate_limit_checks table) with in-memory fallback
- Tighten satellite check enforcement: DB trigger now requires
  overall_status = 'PASS' (not just != 'PENDING')
- Add idempotency keys to prevent offline double-submission
  (SHA-256 hash stored in gold_batches with UNIQUE constraint)
- Fix batch status race condition: update_batch_status trigger now
  checks current status before updating, NEVER overwrites FLAGGED

HIGH fixes:
- Add token-expired detection in offline sync with re-login prompt
- Add Supabase Realtime subscriptions to GoldBod dashboard
- Add null guard + redirect in operator dashboard (prevents info leak)
- Add weight discrepancy warning in refinery dashboard (client-side
  pre-check with confirmation dialog before server submission)
- Fix CSP: remove unsafe-inline in production, keep for dev only

MEDIUM fixes:
- Add sync lock to prevent concurrent SW + main thread sync
- Remove auth token from IndexedDB storage (fetched fresh at sync time)
- Replace detailed error messages with generic ones in all actions/routes
- Add fetch timeout (15s) for offline sync on slow 3G networks
- Remove rate limit reset time from error responses
- Increase minimum weight to 0.0001 kg
- Add node status indicators (filled/empty dots) to operator dashboard

LOW fixes:
- Add composite indexes on hot query paths
- Add operator info panel (name, license, region, status)
- Add HTML tag rejection in Zod schemas (XSS prevention)
- Add Ghana GPS bounding box validation (4.5-11.2°N, -3.3-1.2°E)
- Fix SW activation to notify clients of updates
- Replace inline SW registration script with React component (CSP safe)

New files:
- supabase/migrations/005_security_hardening.sql
- app/api/hmac-secret/route.ts (HMAC secret provisioning)
- components/ServiceWorkerRegistration.tsx

commit.message:
Fix WD detection via ESPN placeholder round analysis

ESPN does not include WD text for players like Rory McIlroy who withdrew
after making the cut. Instead, ESPN adds placeholder linescores for unplayed
rounds (displayValue="-", value=0, no hole-by-hole data). Detect WD by
filtering out placeholder rounds before counting real rounds, then flagging
players with placeholder stubs but fewer real rounds than the field leaders.
Also refactor is_real_ls helper out of the competitor loop.
